name: Advance America
description_short: At 3216 N Main St, Taylor, TX 76574, you'll find Advance America. The phone number is +1 512-352-6649.
description_long:
Advance America is one of the largest consumer lending chains in the United States, operating thousands of physical store locations alongside an online platform. The company describes itself as nationally recognized and fully accredited, serving millions of customers who need short-term cash. They have a significant presence in Florida and across the country, with this particular Jacksonville location serving the Commonwealth Village neighborhood west of downtown.

The company offers three core loan products. Payday Loans range from $100 to $500 and are designed as a two-to-four-week bridge between paychecks. Installment Loans range from $100 to $1,000 and allow repayment across multiple scheduled payments over a longer term, making them more manageable than a lump-sum payoff. Title Loans, offered in partnership with LoanCenter, provide $2,000 to $25,000 using the borrower's paid-off vehicle as collateral. The store also offers Western Union money transfer services and allows customers to apply online, in-store, or by phone for pre-qualification.

Several features distinguish Advance America from smaller independent lenders. The company carries a 4.9 out of 5 average rating drawn from 125,419 verified reviews, with over 200,000 reviews on Google, suggesting consistent service quality at scale. The Jacksonville location offers bilingual service in English and Spanish. Borrowers can start an application online and complete it in-store, offering flexibility. Required documents are straightforward: a government-issued ID, proof of income, a checking account, and a Social Security Number or ITIN.

Honestly, Advance America's products are high-cost by design. Payday loans are among the most expensive forms of credit available — typical APRs run into the triple digits. Title loans carry the additional risk of vehicle repossession if payments are missed, and the LoanCenter partnership means borrowers are dealing with a third party on those larger loans. The store page does not publish APR figures, so borrowers must ask directly. These products serve a real need for consumers who have no other options, but they are not a cost-effective solution for anyone who qualifies for a bank loan, credit union PAL, or credit card advance.
pros: Same-day cash available in-store — no waiting period for funded loans
Three loan types under one roof: payday, installment, and title loans
Title loans reach up to $25,000 — higher ceiling than most payday-focused lenders
4.9/5 average rating from 125,419 verified customer reviews
Bilingual staff (English and Spanish) at this location
Multiple application channels: online, in-store, or phone pre-qualification
Western Union money transfers also available at this location
cons: Payday loan APRs are typically in the triple digits — among the costliest forms of credit
Title loans use your vehicle as collateral; missed payments can result in repossession
No APR or fee disclosures on the store page — costs require a direct inquiry
Payday loans cap at $500, which may be insufficient for many emergency expenses
Title loans processed through third-party partner LoanCenter, not Advance America directly
best_for: Borrowers who need up to $500 by the same day and will repay within two to four weeks
Car owners with a fully paid-off vehicle who need $2,000 or more quickly
Spanish-speaking consumers who prefer bilingual in-person financial assistance
Consumers who have been declined for bank loans or credit cards and need cash immediately
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for consumers facing an immediate, short-term cash shortfall who cannot qualify for lower-cost credit and need funds the same day. The main caveat is that payday loans carry extremely high APRs and title loans risk vehicle repossession — these are last-resort products, not a routine financial tool.

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
